title: Leptinella altilitoralis
intro: Leptinella altilitoralis, also known as New Zealand parsley or small-leaved parsley, is a small, perennial herb native to New Zealand, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as aiding in digestion and reducing stress. Leptinella altilitoralis contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
